summary: Matched fighter translation unit ft_0899 by moving src/melee/ft/ft_0899.c from NonMatching to Matching, adjusting ft_80089B08 local lifetimes and line_len expression shape for MWCC register allocation, relocating ftData_x58_t into shared fighter types, and splitting ft_0899 prototypes into a new header.

key_files.role: Primary matched source file; changed include ownership, made fn_8008998C static bool, reshaped ft_80089B08 line length computation and local scopes, and updated ft_8008A1B8 flags type/expression.
Build configuration changed ft_0899.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Promoted ftData_x58_t from a local typedef in ft_0899.c to shared fighter type declarations and typed ftData.x58 as struct ftData_x58_t*.
New header declaring ft_80089B08 and ft_8008A1B8 with Fighter_GObj and u32 flags signatures.
Removed ft_0899-owned prototypes from neighboring ft_0892 header.
Switched include from ft_0892.h to ft_0899.h for the moved prototype surface.

what_changed: PR body reports ft_80089B08 reached 100.0% match, with main/melee/ft/ft_0899 at 2160/2160 code bytes and the translation unit at 3/3 functions matched.
configure.py now marks melee/ft/ft_0899.c as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
ft_80089B08 line_len calculation changed from dy-first accumulation to dx = sp38.x; dx -= sp2C.x; line_len = dx * dx + dy * dy; followed by a visible `(void) line_len` consumption.
Adjacent line lookup locals were split into narrower scoped next_check/next_id and prev_check/prev_id blocks.
The helper fn_8008998C changed from external s32 declaration/definition shape to a static bool function with a local prototype in ft_0899.c.
ft_8008A1B8 signature changed from int flags to u32 flags, and the bit test was written as `(1 << 2)` instead of `0x4`.
ftData_x58_t was removed from a local typedef in ft_0899.c and added to src/melee/ft/types.h, with ftData.x58 changed from void* to struct ftData_x58_t*.
New src/melee/ft/ft_0899.h owns public prototypes for ft_80089B08 and ft_8008A1B8; callers/includes were updated accordingly.

smart_moves.text: Kept the type recovery in the shared fighter type header instead of leaving ftData_x58_t local to ft_0899.c, allowing ftData.x58 to use a typed pointer rather than void*.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: local typedef removed from ft_0899.c
diff_excerpt: ftData.x58 typed and struct ftData_x58_t added in types.h

smart_moves.text: Moved ft_0899-owned declarations out of ft_0892.h into a new ft_0899.h and updated includes in fighter.c and ftaction.c.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fighter.c and ftaction.c include changes

smart_moves.text: Documented verification commands and the specific MWCC register-allocation issue in the PR body, making the matching tactic auditable.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For MWCC FPR allocation around fused multiply-add sequences, expression shape can matter: in this PR, writing `line_len = dx * dx + dy * dy` as a single add, while keeping `line_len` visibly consumed, was reported to select the target `fmuls`/`fmadds` registers without an extra move.
Narrowing local scopes and local lifetimes can be a matching lever for GPR allocation; this PR explicitly cites local variable lifetimes and introduces smaller next/prev line-id scopes.
When matching exposes a reusable structure behind a void pointer, promote the recovered struct to the owning shared type header and type the field directly when evidence supports it.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftData.x58 changed from void* to struct ftData_x58_t*

naming_conventions.convention: New header for the ft_0899 translation unit was named `ft_0899.h`, and the include guard used address-style text `GALE01_08A1B8`.
naming_conventions.notes: The PR evidence shows this naming choice but does not establish it as a global header-guard convention.

naming_conventions.convention: Public functions kept address-style names such as `ft_80089B08` and `ft_8008A1B8`; the internal helper kept `fn_8008998C` and was made static.
naming_conventions.notes: Consistent with conservative naming where semantics are not proven by the PR slice.

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary matching locals were named for nearby roles, including `line_len`, `next_check`, `next_id`, `prev_check`, and `prev_id`.
naming_conventions.notes: Evidence is local to this function.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reshape `dx` computation into separate assignment/subtraction before computing `line_len`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_effect: Part of local lifetime/expression shaping used for target GPR/FPR allocation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a single expression `line_len = dx * dx + dy * dy` rather than two-step accumulation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_effect: PR body says this produced target `fmuls`/`fmadds` register choice without adding an extra move.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add `(void) line_len;` after the expression to keep the local visibly consumed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_effect: PR body ties visible local consumption to target register allocation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use inner blocks for next/prev line-id checks to shorten or separate local variable lifetimes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_effect: PR body broadly says local variable lifetimes were adjusted for MWCC allocation.

review_feedback.text: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the loaded PR slice. The only issue comments were a decomp-dev bot match report and the author's short celebratory comment.

fn_8008998C static bool IKState Vec3 normal

notes: The PR body provides explicit rationale for the line_len expression and local lifetime changes, and the diff shows the exact source edits.
The loaded slice contains no review comments, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
Verification commands and match percentages are author/bot-reported from the PR slice; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The source diff shows PAD_STACK(16) present at the end of ft_80089B08, but the excerpt does not show it as newly introduced; it should not be treated as a PR-added tactic without further evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Single-expression squared-length computation can affect MWCC fmuls/fmadds register choice
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2753, the author reports the final ft_80089B08 mismatch was an FPR allocation issue around `dx * dx + dy * dy`, not scheduling. The diff changed two-step accumulation into `line_len = dx * dx + dy * dy` and added visible local consumption, which the PR body says selected the target `fmuls`/`fmadds` register choice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local lifetime shaping used to influence MWCC allocation in ft_80089B08
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2753 states that local variable lifetimes were adjusted so MWCC selected target GPR/FPR allocation. The diff narrows next/prev line-id locals into inner blocks and splits `dx` assignment/subtraction before using it in `line_len`.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow expression-shape and visible-consumption tactics for MWCC register allocation
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2753 as an example under matching tactics: for a fused multiply-add-like squared-length computation, `line_len = dx * dx + dy * dy` plus visible consumption of the local was reported to fix FPR allocation without an extra move. Keep this framed as evidence-required and local, not a general style preference.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete, verified matching tactic that fits the existing evidence-required matching-tactics standard.

source_update_candidates.title: ft_0899.c matched in PR #2753 via line_len/local-lifetime shaping
source_update_candidates.text: For src/melee/ft/ft_0899.c, PR #2753 matched ft_80089B08 and moved the TU to Matching. The notable tactic was reshaping `line_len` as `dx * dx + dy * dy`, adding visible local consumption, and narrowing next/prev locals to influence MWCC allocation.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is path-specific matching history likely useful for future work in the same fighter TU.

source_update_candidates.title: ftData_x58_t ownership moved to fighter types in PR #2753
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2753 removed a local ftData_x58_t typedef from src/melee/ft/ft_0899.c, added the struct to src/melee/ft/types.h, added a forward typedef in src/melee/ft/forward.h, and changed ftData.x58 from void* to struct ftData_x58_t*.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: types.h struct and field change
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff supports a concrete type-ownership update that may help future typed-field cleanup.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a global rule that `(void) local;` should be added for matching; this PR only supports it as a narrow tactic with explicit objdiff/build evidence.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale beyond merge status; no review comments are loaded.
Do not treat the include guard name `GALE01_08A1B8` as an accepted global convention from this PR alone.
Do not claim PAD_STACK(16) was introduced by this PR; the diff only shows it remaining near the function end.
